ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for doner kebab sandwich; mapped to generic sandwich food product (FOODON_00002473) as closest defensible parent anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Sandwich) used as closest defensible parent category; no doner-specific FoodEx2 code exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for doner sandwich; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 300 g, comprising a filled doner sandwich (bread, kebab meat, salad, yogurt sauce) plus a side of french fries.

nutrient_source.title: Doner Sandwich — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Doner Sandwich (cr_id G00010170). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entry (USDA #21065, Lamb, ground, raw) cross-referenced with HPB total fat value; see notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — white bread reference; Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a doner sandwich as a composite dish. GI of 72 is estimated from the dominant carbohydrate vehicle: white bread (GI ~70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The sandwich is white-bread dominant (58.4g carb/100g, fibre 2.3g/100g). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 175.14) / 100 ≈ 126.1, using 300g serving with 58.38g carb/100g (175.14g carb per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 801 mg/100g — far exceeds the 600 mg/100g upper threshold; a 300g serving delivers approximately 2403 mg sodium, exceeding the entire recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid doner sandwiches due to extremely high sodium content (801 mg/100g); if consumed, limit to a small portion, omit sauces, and compensate by eliminating all other sodium sources for the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (58.4g/100g) with low dietary fibre (2.3g/100g) and high GI (~72) creates a very high glycaemic load; a 300g serving delivers approximately 175g carbohydrate, far exceeding a single-meal carbohydrate budget.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this dish or strictly limit portion size to no more than 100g, substitute wholemeal bread, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the glycaemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (24.54g/100g) with estimated saturated fat ~7.8g/100g, trans fat ~0.2g/100g, and sodium 801 mg/100g combine to create an atherogenic and hypertensive dietary profile unfavourable for cardiovascular health.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid doner sandwiches due to the combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium; if consumed occasionally, choose chicken over lamb, omit sauces, and limit to a half-portion.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~7.8g/100g), trans fat (~0.2g/100g), arachidonic acid from red meat, and sodium 801 mg/100g create a strongly pro-inflammatory dietary profile.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this dish; the combination of red meat, refined carbohydrate, trans fat, and high sodium is among the most pro-inflammatory fast-food profiles available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A Doner Sandwich is a popular fast-food dish sold widely in Singapore and internationally, consisting of sliced or shaved doner kebab meat (chicken or lamb, cooked on a vertical rotisserie) served inside or alongside white bread, accompanied by fresh tomato, onion, lettuce, and a yogurt-based sauce, with french fries on the side. The bread is typically a soft white roll or flatbread. The meat is heavily seasoned with Middle Eastern spices including cumin, paprika, garlic, and coriander, contributing to the dish's high sodium content (801 mg/100g). The overall nutritional profile is energy-dense at 494 kcal/100g, with high carbohydrate (58.4g/100g) from refined white bread and fries, moderate-to-high fat (24.54g/100g) from the kebab meat and sauce, and moderate protein (11.47g/100g). Dietary fibre is low at 2.3g/100g. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high), driven by the white bread base, making this dish unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. A standard 300g serving delivers approximately 1482 kcal, 175g carbohydrate, and 2403 mg sodium. Culturally, the doner sandwich is of Turkish-Middle Eastern origin and has been widely adopted in Singapore as a street food and fast-food staple, available at halal-certified outlets. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner meal.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a doner sandwich?
Is doner sandwich high in sodium?
What is the GI of a doner sandwich?
Is doner sandwich good for weight loss?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010170) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous lamb/chicken kebab entries. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database white bread reference as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle. All values are internally consistent and cross-validated.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (7.8g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for ground lamb (USDA #21065) and chicken kebab, cross-referenced with HPB total fat (24.54g/100g); not directly reported in HPB source. 2. Trans fat (0.2g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for lamb-based kebab products; not directly reported in HPB source. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not available in HPB Singapore Foodo database for this composite dish. 4. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish and no sufficiently close approved-source composite entry was available to supply defensible values. 5. cholesterol_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source; confirmed zero per HPB Foodo database (G00010170). Note: this value appears anomalous for a meat-containing dish and may reflect a data gap in the original HPB record rather than a true zero; downstream users should treat with caution. 6. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white bread as the dominant carbohydrate vehicle (University of Sydney GI Database); no direct GI measurement for doner sandwich as a composite dish was found in approved sources. 7. Halal status is flagged as likely true for Singapore HPB-listed doner sandwich outlets but cannot be guaranteed for all vendors; users should verify at point of purchase. 8. Ontology: foodon_match_type and foodex2_match_type set to 'closest_parent' as no exact ontology term exists for doner sandwich specifically; FOODON_00002473 (sandwich food product) and FoodEx2 A0FKP (Sandwich) are the closest defensible parent categories.
